claims.subject: Ahmad Rashid
claims.evidence: ticket bouncing. every time a merchant complains about something showing up wrong in the app — reconciliation off, transaction missing, receipt weird — the ticket comes in to cs, cs sends it to mobile because the merchant saw it on a phone, mobile looks and says it's a backend issue and sends it to payments, payments looks and says it's actually a cs configuration issue and sends it back. that loop takes days.
claims.object_value: ticket bouncing between CS, mobile, and payments teams — loop takes days

claims.subject: Ahmad Rashid
claims.evidence: github, linear, datadog for the backend bits we own, firebase crashlytics for mobile, slack. nothing exotic.
claims.object_value: GitHub, Linear, Datadog, Firebase Crashlytics, Slack

claims.subject: Ahmad Rashid
claims.evidence: we don't own the migration but we consume merchant_id in the pos apis so we'll be one of the dependent services that has to repoint after cutover.
claims.object_value: dependent service — consumes merchant_id in POS APIs, must repoint after cutover

claims.subject: Ahmad Rashid
claims.evidence: the ticket triage thing i just said. that's the big one for me. the secondary one is i want to understand which app behaviors are actually generating tickets. i suspect a small number of behaviors are generating most of the support load and if i knew which ones i could prioritize the fixes properly.
claims.object_value: AI ticket triage correlating ticket text with crash data and backend error logs; and identifying which app behaviors generate most support load

claims.subject: Ahmad Rashid
claims.evidence: i'd want a system that can look at a ticket and tell me which app behavior actually caused it.
